auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
economic_notes: First Nation derives revenue from gaming services, postal services, Musselwhite Mine, and Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership. Various investments in numbered companies including Happy Time Tours, Happy Time Leasing, Wasaya Airways, BBH Limited Partnership, Asheweig Winter Roads Corporation, and others. COVID-19 impacted operations in March 2020 with additional funding received for wages and health and safety costs.
notes: Audit is heavily qualified on multiple bases: (1) incomplete tangible capital asset inventory not fully adopted per PS 3150; (2) physical inventories not observed; (3) investments in various entities accounted for by modified equity method with unaudited financial information and missing summary disclosures; (4) gaming revenues and expenses lack controls over cash receipts; (5) no budget information disclosed. Financial statements signed November 8, 2023 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2020 — approximately 3.5 year delay. Province is Ontario (Thunder Bay). Auditor firm name not clearly identified, only described as Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants. ISC funding recognized per deferred revenue note ($11,682,115); total government transfers calculated as ISC ($11,738,771) + Provincial ($1,649,168) = $13,387,939. OSR includes Ontario First Nations LP, Musselwhite Mine, Gaming, Postal fees, Other. Kingfisher Lake Social-Economic Development Corporation is the wholly-owned GBE accounted for by modified equity.
